## Endpoints NOT Covered by Dedicated Scenarios

---

## API Behavior: mock — Adaptations Made

Because JSONPlaceholder is a non-persistent fake API, the following adaptations were applied:

| Standard Guardrail | Adaptation Applied |
|--------------------|--------------------|
| POST → GET (verify created resource exists) | Not asserted — POST returns id=101 always (faked). GET uses stable id=1 instead. |
| DELETE → GET 404 (verify deletion) | Not asserted — DELETE succeeds but resource still exists on next GET. |
| PUT/PATCH → GET (verify mutation persisted) | Not asserted — mutation responses are accepted as-is per mock contract. |
| POST with missing required fields → 400 | Documents 201 (permissive mock behavior); notes production expectation. |
| DELETE non-existent → 404 | Documents 200 (JSONPlaceholder behavior); notes production expectation. |

---

## Recommendations

1. **Execution order for smoke run:** posts-crud-happy-path → posts-list-filter → posts-comments
2. **Regression run:** all 5 scenarios in parallel (no shared state — mock API is stateless)
3. **If migrating to a persistent backend:** Update `## API Behavior` from `mock` to `live` in all files. Steps 8–12 in posts-negative.md (POST validation) will need expected status updated to 400.
4. **Auth note:** No authentication required. If the API adds security, add Bearer token steps and `{{ENV.API_TOKEN}}` to the Application section.
